The one thing Brian wanted to achieve with this proposal was the element of surprise. If you know us, you know how challenging that is! Not only do we live together, but we live in the tiniest of tiny homes, so hiding anything is difficult. The biggest roadblock I expected was the cost of an engagement ring (though I have told Brian in the past that I really don’t care about the ring). I had NO idea my grandmother’s ring had been sitting in my sister’s house for the past several weeks, waiting for this exact moment! Brian wanted to ensure the most magical experience possible while also surprising me. So, not only did he carefully guide me towards this beach adventure without me growing suspicious, but he also created a backup surprise! A very special video… Read on! title: Joeal Wolfe
text: Joeal’s lifelong passion for horses has evolved into a cherished family tradition. Now a devoted mom, she’s sharing her love for these majestic animals with her two horse-crazy daughters. Joeal strongly advocates holistic care for people and animals, and she’s thrilled to be part of a company that mirrors her values. Recently, Joeal and her family traded the rugged landscapes of Northern BC for the sunny shores of the Sunshine Coast, where they’re fully embracing coastal life. When she’s not at the barn, you’ll likely find her and her family soaking up the ocean’s beauty! [ROLE] Customer Experience Expert

title: Cavallo Hoof Boots in Water
h1: Cavallo Hoof Boots in Mud & Water
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots in Mud & Water Cavallo Hoof Boots perform exceptionally well in water, mud, slushy snow and other wet conditions, due to their patented, performance-driven design. The boots feature side drainage holes and a cone-shaped upper that mirrors a horse's hoof, further preventing suction in muddy or submerged conditions. They allow water to flow out freely while keeping debris outside of the boot. Because of this, riders trust Cavallo in streams, rivers, mud, and even ocean water. The boots stay secure, protect the hoof, and maintain traction in slippery environments. Table of Contents Effective Water Drainage Using Cavallo Hoof Boots in Wet Conditions Riding in Water With Cavallo Hoof Boots Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Cavallo Hoof Boots Offer Effective Water Drainage Cavallo Hoof Boots provide excellent drainage in water and mud due to their thoughtfully engineered side slits. The patented drainage holes are built into the lower sides of each boot. Because of this, as your horse moves through water, the slits instantly allow moisture and water to drain. This prevents pooling and allows the boot to stay lightweight, even when fully submerged. Conversely, boots that hold water can cause rubbing, heat buildup, or instability for your horse. Cavallo’s side slits easily solve this problem. By allowing water to exit quickly with every step, debris

title: Using Cavallo Hoof Boots on Left and Right Feet
h1: Using Cavallo Hoof Boots on Left and Right Feet: What You Need To Know
text: Using Cavallo Hoof Boots on Left and Right Feet: What You Need To Know Using Cavallo Hoof Boots on left and right feet is simple, as most Cavallo boot styles are designed to work on either hoof! Moreover, this symmetry makes them incredibly convenient for horse owners. You can swap the boots as needed without losing performance, comfort, or protection. Because Cavallo boots are interchangeable, you can rotate them between feet to reduce uneven wear. This helps extend the life of your gear. Still, some riders prefer to assign one boot to the left hoof and one to the right. Why? It helps you track wear patterns over time. You might notice that your horse drags one foot or wears down one side more. Assigning a left and right boot supports a custom fit. It’s optional, but many riders find it offers even more comfort and performance over time! Table of Contents Can Cavallo Hoof Boots Be Used on Both Feet? Benefits of Interchangeable Cavallo Hoof Boots Tips for Marking Your Hoof Boots Left and Right (If You Want To!) Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Can Cavallo Hoof Boots Be Used on Both Feet? Yes! Most Cavallo Hoof Boots are designed to fit either the left or right hoof. title: May 2025 Newsletter - Happy Mother's Day!
text: To all the fabulous Horse Mamas out there—Happy Mother’s Day! This Mother's Day we are celebrating your fierce love, boundless compassion, and the tireless care you give, not just to your human families, but to your beloved equine partners as well. Being a mother means nurturing those who need us most, and that same instinct shines through every time you tend to a horse in pain, offer comfort during a hoof injury, or search for ways to ease their discomfort. Your heart knows no bounds, and your dedication to helping your horses heal and thrive is a beautiful extension of your mothering spirit. Here's to you—the caregivers, the hoof helpers, and the heroes in the barn and beyond! Here are Some Inspiring Stories of Horse Mamas Using Cavallos to Rehab, Recover & Ride their Beloved Equines: "Whether you’re riding or rehabbing, these boots deliver!" "Outstanding for riding & rehabilitation, Cavallo horse boots are hands down the best hoof boots I’ve ever used. They’re incredibly secure, sturdy, and have proven reliable time and time again. I’ve used them extensively for laminitis rehabilitation, abscesses, and seedy toe, and they’ve consistently provided excellent protection and comfort for my horse. They stay in place during rides and hold up beautifully even with regular use. I always highly recommend them to fellow horse owners and am more than happy to show them off in action. W

title: Cavallo Hoof Boots with Feathers
h1: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Horses with Feathers
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Horses with Feathers Cavallo Hoof Boots for Horses with Feathers, such as Gypsy Vanners, Friesians, and Shires, provide unmatched comfort and protection. Designed to fit well on heavier-boned horses, Cavallo Hoof Boots provide a protective fit that keeps them secure while riding. With a bit of practice, managing your horse's feathers during the booting process is simple, allowing every horse to benefit from the protection and comfort that Cavallo Hoof Boots provide. If your horse has feathers, Cavallo boots are a smart and stylish solution for ensuring protection, traction, and everyday hoof comfort. Table of Contents Using Cavallo Hoof Boots with Feathers Benefits of Cavallo Hoof Boots Tips and Tricks for Feathered Legs Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Using Cavallo Hoof Boots with Feathers Booting up a horse with feathers is easy! To put the boots on, hold the feathers aside with one hand and slide the boot over the hoof. Alternatively, you can place the boot directly over both the feathers and the hoof together - both methods are effective. Before securing your Velcro closures, ensure that your horse's feathers are lying flat and are not tangled. This will keep your horse comfortable and help the boot stay in place. Always check that no long hairs are pinched or caught. Take your time during the first few attempts. You'll quickly dev

title: Deanna Stoppler
text: Deanna is a horse and animal lover who grew up in Alberta, Canada, and started working with horses when she was 9 years old. She attended farrier school in 2012 and began a full-time farrier business after graduation. Within a few years of practicing the craft of farriery, Deanna noticed the detrimental effects that horseshoes were having on hooves. Deanna decided to stop shoeing horses, sold her farrier rig and equipment, and began her journey relearning hoof care from the barefoot perspective. Cavallo Hoof Boots quickly became a staple in Deanna’s hoof care business, helping rehab horses out of shoes to barefoot. Deanna joined the Cavallo team in 2025 and shares her passion and enthusiasm for keeping horses comfortable and sound, barefoot and booted, with our valued Cavallo customers. Deanna lives with her lifelong partner, Matt, and his two kids, their three dogs, miniature horse, and two goats on a small hobby farm in northern Vermont. [ROLE] Customer Experience Expert

title: Cleaning Cavallo Hoof Boots
text: Cleaning Your Cavallo Hoof Boots Cleaning Cavallo Hoof Boots is quick and easy, thanks to their low-maintenance, high-performance design. Consequently, you can rinse Cavallo boots with a hose or clean them simply by riding through water; no special tools are required. This makes daily cleaning effortless for busy horse owners. When a more thorough cleaning is required, a mild antibacterial soap can effectively disinfect the boots without damaging the materials. Cavallo Hoof Boots are built to last, but routine cleaning helps maintain their fit, comfort, and appearance over time. Best of all, several Cavallo boot styles, including the Trek, ELB, CLB, and BFB, are machine washable at temperatures of up to 30°C. Simply toss them in your washing machine for a deeper refresh when needed. Whether you’re removing trail dust, scrubbing away mud, or disinfecting after turnout, cleaning Cavallo Hoof Boots is always simple, effective, and stress-free. title: Where The Rubber Meets The Road: Alternatives to Metal Horseshoes
text: Our purpose is to discuss the application of horse boots as an alternative to metal shoes. The idea that horses can be barefoot is not new. Horses have a long history of barefoot performance and have carried fully armoured, full sized men into battle. They have been used for fieldwork, war and performance in their natural barefoot state. When the horse’s weight descends, the hoof is sandwiched between that load and the ground. It is meant to spread apart upon weight bearing, with the coffin bone dropping down like a trampoline. This is the natural shock-absorbing feature of the hoof. The walls spread apart (up to 10mm from side to side) and the sole draws flat. Horses with this elasticity and hoof function are most adequately prepared to absorb shock and concussion. When metal is nailed in all around, how does the hoof perform its duty? Where is the shock absorbed? Perhaps it’s absorbed in the sensitive tissue of the hoof or further up the structure of the leg. Perhaps the market proliferation of products containing glucosamines,MSM and anti inflammatories are really an indication of our inadequate understanding of the shock-absorbing features of the hoof. Perhaps if we allow our horses to function naturally they would not be showing increasing symptoms of pain and discomfort. The metal shoe is nailed on when the hoof is in the air. It is at its smallest, most contracted shape.

title: Treating Thrush with Cavallo Hoof Boots
h1: Treating Thrush with Cavallo Hoof Boots
text: Treating Thrush with Cavallo Hoof Boots Thrush is one of the most common hoof conditions in horses, yet it is often misunderstood. Thrush develops when poor hoof function, limited circulation, and environmental factors allow bacteria and fungi to thrive. Effective management focuses on supporting healthy tissue, not destroying it. Correct trimming, consistent movement, balanced nutrition, and thoughtful environmental care all play critical roles. Treating thrush with Cavallo Hoof Boots adds practical support by protecting the hoof, keeping treatments in place, allowing soaking, and improving comfort on challenging footing. When combined with a whole-horse approach, Cavallo Hoof Boots help create healthier, more resilient hooves that resist infection. Table of Contents What Is Thrush in Horses? Safe and Effective Thrush Treatment Thrush Treatment and Prevention with Cavallo Hoof Boots Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots What Is Thrush in Horses? Thrush is a bacterial and fungal infection that affects horses’ hooves. It can cause dark discharge, deep cracks extending between the heel bulbs, and a strong odor. Many horses also experience hoof sensitivity and pain. Thrush is often mistaken for a hygiene issue. In reality, it develops when the hoof environment allows harmful organisms to thrive. Most thrush-causing microbes are anaerobic. This means they prefer dark,

title: Cavallo in the Media
text: Beyoncé: Queen of Hoof Boots Did you know Beyoncé rocked more than just the stage at the iconic NFL "Beyoncé Bowl"? In a dazzling twist that had the equestrian world buzzing, Cavallo Trek Boots made a surprise appearance—on her horse! Yes, you read that right. The same boots trusted by trail riders and performance horses alike were a prominent part of Beyoncé's entrance. But how did Cavallo Hoof Boots end up under the spotlight of one of the biggest stages in the world? And what does this mean for your own horse's performance, comfort, and style? Read on to uncover the story behind this even, and find out why your horse might just benefit from a superstar upgrade! How did it happen? An excited customer shared the new American Eagle video ad featuring Ella Langley, and there’s a detail that’s hard to miss: the horse she’s riding is wearing Cavallo Hoof Boots! Ella is known to love horses, which raises an intriguing question…could that be her own horse in the ad? It’s something to feel proud of: the same boots trusted on your horses are also chosen for big productions.
